hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: 37th percentile; overheal (53.2%) sat comfortably under the sample's worst parse (58.4%), and HPM (3.32) ran at 0.63x the sample average. By far the deadliest kill of the raid - 15 deaths, the most of any kill this raid night.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (6.4%) undershot the sample's 30.4% share by the widest margin so far; Renew (31.0%) and Power Word: Shield (19.0%) both ran well ahead of the sample instead.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Potion went unused against a 59% sample usage rate. Blessing of Life was cast once, self-targeted - a real, if rare-in-sample, use. With 15 deaths this kill, the nearest-cooldown timestamps all trail that single Blessing of Life cast by 41-98 seconds - too far, and Blessing of Life is a self-targeted buff rather than a save for another player, so none of these read as a real missed opportunity.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (89.7%) ran well above the sample average (71.0%) - almost all healing landed on the top 5 recipients - while the top target (Captinhelp, 28.7%) took a share close to the sample's typical concentration (37.3%).

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: 30th percentile; HPS (749) ran at 0.32x the sample average, and overheal (36.6%) sat just under the sample's worst parse (40.5%). The second-deadliest kill of the raid at 12 deaths.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (21.2%) undershot the sample's dominant 71.2% share by the widest margin of the raid.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra (2 casts), and Mana Potion (2 casts) all landed in line with the sample; Blessing of Life was cast once, self-targeted. Of the 12 deaths this kill, the closest trails a Mana Potion cast by just 209 milliseconds - but Mana Potion restores the caster's own mana rather than protecting another player, so even this tight timing doesn't read as a real missed save.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: The most tank-concentrated kill of the raid: Captinhelp took 36.3% of healing, more than triple the sample's typical top-target share (11.9%), with coverage (69.1%) also well above the sample average (45.7%).

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: The strongest kill of the raid by percentile (53rd); overheal (46.7%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (38.4%) by 8 points, and HPM (4.44) ran at 0.45x the sample average, among the lowest ratios of the raid. Ten deaths this kill, the third-highest of the raid.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (36.2%) undershot the sample's 77.3% share; Prayer of Mending (24.6%) and Flash Heal (20.5%) both ran well ahead of the sample to compensate.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Potion went unused against an 81% sample usage rate - the clearest single cooldown gap of this kill. Blessing of Life was cast once, self-targeted. The ten deaths split into two clusters, the closest trailing a Blessing of Life cast by about 2 seconds, but Blessing of Life is a self-targeted buff, not a save for another player, so this reads as timing coincidence rather than a real missed opportunity.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (50.9%) ran above the sample average (39.7%); top-target concentration (18.1%, Captinhelp) landed in line.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: 48th percentile; HPM (5.52, 0.7x the sample average) was the most efficient ratio of the raid so far, and overheal (43.0%) landed exactly at the sample's worst parse. Active time (98.2%) was in line, and only one death this kill.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (37.3%) undershot the sample's 63.5% share; Renew (21.7%) and Prayer of Mending (19.7%) both ran ahead of the sample instead.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra (2 casts), and Mana Potion all landed in line with the sample; Blessing of Life was cast once, self-targeted. The one death this kill (Megatauro) trails the nearest Shadowfiend cast by 17 seconds, too far to read as related.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Captinhelp took the largest healing share (32.0%), well above the sample's typical top-target concentration (16.4%) - a real, tank-heavy kill.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: 32nd percentile; overheal (48.9%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (45.4%) by 3.5 points, and HPM (4.38) ran at 0.6x the sample average. Three deaths this kill.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (33.8%) undershot the sample's 58.4% share; Prayer of Mending (23.5%) ran well ahead of the sample's 8.4%.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion all landed in line with the sample; Blessing of Life was cast twice, both self-targeted. None of the three deaths this kill land near a real save-type cooldown - the nearest casts (Blessing of Life, Chakra, Mana Potion) are all self-targeted resource/buff tools.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: The most tank-concentrated kill of the raid: Captinhelp took 50.3% of healing, close to triple the sample's typical top-target share (17.0%), with coverage (70.4%) also well above the sample average (44.0%).

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: 48th percentile; HPM (4.87) ran at 0.77x the sample average, the most efficient ratio of the raid, and overheal (43.4%) sat comfortably below this boss's unusually high sample worst-parse (74.6%). Six deaths this kill, two of them Lasmur's own, 15 seconds apart.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (28.5%) ran close to the sample's 29.8% share, one of the smallest composition gaps of the raid; Prayer of Mending (16.2%) ran ahead of the sample's 8.6% to make up the small remaining difference.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra (2 casts), and Mana Potion all landed in line with the sample; Blessing of Life was cast once, self-targeted. Neither of Lasmur's own two deaths lines up with a real save opportunity - the nearest cooldown (Shadowfiend, cast late in the fight) trails them by 32-47 seconds, and Shadowfiend restores mana rather than preventing death regardless.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (58.9%) ran above the sample average (41.4%); top-target concentration (20.7%, Braila) landed in line with the sample.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: 43rd percentile; overheal (56.1%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (46.7%) by 9 points, and HPM (3.3) ran at 0.56x the sample average. Four deaths this kill.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (18.4%) undershot the sample's 42.6% share; Renew (30.5%) and Prayer of Mending (19.6%) both ran well ahead of the sample instead.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Mana Potion went unused against a 96% sample usage rate - the highest benchmark usage rate of any cooldown flagged this raid. This was also the only kill this raid with no Blessing of Life cast at all (2% sample usage, so not itself a real gap). One death trails a Chakra cast by just 1.9 seconds, but Chakra is self-targeted, so even this close timing doesn't read as a real missed save; the other three deaths trail Chakra/Shadowfiend casts by 28-59 seconds.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Rolopally took the largest healing share (40.4%), well above the sample's typical top-target concentration (18.9%), with coverage (71.9%) also running above the sample average (49.8%).

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: 41st percentile; HPS (2213, 0.59x the sample average) and target coverage (40.7% vs. a 37.7% sample average) were both close to sample-normal - the smallest target-spread gap of the raid. Overheal (31.1%) exceeded this boss's tight worst-parse ceiling (23.5%) by 8 points despite being one of the lower absolute overheal numbers this raid. Two deaths this kill.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing (79.1%) ran close to the sample's 81.9% share - the smallest real composition gap of the raid; the largest listed gap is Prayer of Healing, a spell never cast here that the sample uses on 6.0% of parses.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra (2 casts), and Mana Potion all landed in line with the sample; Blessing of Life was cast twice, both self-targeted. Both deaths this kill trail Blessing of Life casts closely (950 milliseconds and 6.7 seconds), but Blessing of Life buffs the caster, not the dying player, so neither reads as a real missed save despite the tight timing.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (40.7%) and top-target concentration (9.6%, Braila) both landed close to the sample average - the least concentrated, most evenly-spread kill of the raid.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: The lowest percentile of the raid (22nd); overheal (67.6%) was the highest absolute overheal of any kill this raid, exceeding the sample's worst parse (62.2%) by 5 points, and HPM (2.88) ran at 0.48x the sample average. The only kill this raid with zero deaths.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash Heal (13.7%) undershot the sample's 42.1% share, the largest gap this kill; Renew (27.5%) ran well ahead of the sample's 2.6% to compensate.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Shadowfiend, Chakra, and Mana Potion all landed in line with the sample; Blessing of Life was cast once, self-targeted. No deaths this kill, so no cooldown-timing question to check.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (41.9%) and top-target concentration (16.4%, Captinhelp) both landed in line with the sample - the low HPS/HPM/overheal numbers above are the real story on this kill, not target spread.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: not fully consistent - Main Hand differs across kills and is flagged for review
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: The Main Hand variant doesn't read as a clean single-kill swap - worth confirming whether it was a deliberate weapon change or a real equipping gap on some kills. All 9 enchantable slots carry a permanent enchant, and all 9 kills had a complete consumable setup.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: This was a difficult raid night overall: Hydross the Unstable (15 deaths), The Lurker Below (12), and Leotheras the Blind (10) accounted for the bulk of the raid's death toll, and every kill except Al'ar had at least one death. None of the nearby cooldown timings point to a real missed save anywhere this raid - Blessing of Life, Chakra, Shadowfiend, and Mana Potion are all self-targeted resource or buff tools, not defensive cooldowns aimed at another player, even on the handful of kills where a death landed within a second or two of one of these casts. Blessing of Life was cast on 8 of 9 kills (all except Al'ar), the most consistent cooldown pattern of the raid, against a Top 100 sample that uses it on only 3-8% of parses per boss. Circle of Healing undershot the sample on most kills, closest to matching on Void Reaver (79.1% vs. 81.9%) and Solarian; Power Word: Shield's own near-0% Top 100 usage rate on most bosses is a known benchmark bias, not a real norm, so its low usage isn't read as underuse anywhere in this report. Lasmur self-died twice on Lady Vashj, 15 seconds apart - the only kill with a self-death this raid. The weakest kill by percentile was Solarian (22nd, also the only kill with zero deaths); the strongest was Leotheras the Blind (53rd).
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: This was a difficult raid night overall - Hydross the Unstable (15 deaths), The Lurker Below (12), and Leotheras the Blind (10) accounted for the bulk of the raid's death toll, though none of the nearby cooldown timings point to a real missed save. Blessing of Life was cast on 8 of 9 kills, the most consistent cooldown pattern of the raid.
